Job Description:

We are searching for a colleague to join our Envista Orthodontics HR Team to execute people strategy within our Design and Commercial team based mostly in Madrid. HRBP is responsible for aligning business objectives with employees and management. The position formulates partnerships across the HR function to deliver value-added service to management and employees that reflects the business objectives of the organization. In addition, the HR Business Partner may take a leading role in responsibilities including but not limited to employee relations, works council relations (if required), on-boarding and exit processes, training and development, compensation and benefits administration and talent acquisition.

Accountabilities:

Partners with the senior leaders of the assigned departments to understand and execute the organization’s human resource and talent strategy particularly as it relates to current and future talent needs, recruiting, retention, performance management and succession planning.
Collaborates with leaders accros Europe to optimize business success through HR initiatives, such as the annual Engagement cycle
Analyzes trends in compensation and benefits; researches and proposes competitive base and incentive pay programs to ensure the organization attracts and retains top talent.
Creates learning and development programs and initiatives that provide internal development opportunities for employees.
Oversees employee disciplinary meetings, terminations, and investigations.
Provides strategic advice, support, counsel and services to internal business partners, and external audiences.
Trains and gives presentations to groups of employees on various topics including policies and programs.
Maintains compliance with local employment laws and regulations, and recommended best practices; reviews policies and practices to maintain compliance..

Job Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree required, preferably in HR, Business Management, or related field.
Experience partnering with leaders at the Sr. Director level, in hiring and developing high performing teams.
5-8 years’ experience working in Human Resources field in roles of increasing responsibility.
Proven, strong employee collaboration and partnership experience required.
Working knowledge of recruiting, data capturing, compensation, benefits, and training.
Analytical skills using Excel.
Strong effective communication skills oral/written.
Fluent in English
Ability to set and follow process, but also comfortable deviating from process when it makes sense.
Ability to cope with fast pace and ambiguous environment.

Operating Company:

Ormco

Ormco is a global leader and innovator of high-quality orthodontic products and solutions, including brackets and wires. For more than 60 years, our team has partnered with the orthodontic community to help create over 20 million smiles in more than 140 countries. We build trusted relationships. Each one is rooted in respect and understanding. We take that approach when we help orthodontists achieve their clinical and practice management objectives. We take the same approach when we help our team bring their personal best to work each day, ready to make a difference and reach their full potential.

Job Description:

An opportunity for Talent Acquisition has arisen within Airbus Defence & Space in Getafe (Madrid).
The Mission: Finding the minds that will take us beyond the horizon.
Why Airbus?

At Airbus Defence and Space, we don’t just build aircraft and satellites; we build the future of global security and exploration. As our Talent Acquisition Partner , you aren’t just "filling talent." You are the architect of our teams, identifying the pioneers, engineers, and visionaries who will solve the challenges of tomorrow.

RESPONSABILITIES

You will lead journey for our most critical asset: People. You are a strategic consultant to our hiring managers and a brand ambassador to the world.

Be a Talent Scout: Move beyond LinkedIn. You’ll design creative sourcing strategies to find "purple squirrels" in the aerospace and tech sectors.

The Candidate Advocate: You’ll craft a world-class experience, ensuring every candidate—hired or not—becomes a fan of Airbus.

Strategic Partner: You won't just run interviews. You’ll advise hiring managers on market trends, salary benchmarks, and diversity best practices.

Brand Storyteller: You’ll represent us at major events and career fairs, sharing what it’s really like to work at the heart of European innovation.

SKILLS

A Seasoned Recruiter: You have 3+ years of experience, ideally in high-tech, defence, or aerospace.

A Sourcing Ninja: You navigate LinkedIn Recruiter, talent pools, and professional networks like a pro.

A Connector: You build trust easily with everyone from junior engineers to senior executives.

Tech-Savvy: You’ve mastered the ATS (if you know Workday , that’s a huge plus!).

Multilingual: You are fluent in Spanish and English . In our world, communication knows no borders.

Compliance-Conscious: You understand the nuances of Spanish labor law to ensure a smooth and fair hiring process.

What’s in it for You?

Innovation at Scale: Work in an environment where your hires literally change how the world moves and stays safe.

Global Reach: Collaborate with international teams and learn from the best in the industry.

Growth: We invest in our people just as much as our tech.

WHICH BENEFITS WILL YOU HAVE AS AIRBUS EMPLOYEE?
At Airbus we are focused on our employees and their welfare. Take a look at some of our social benefits:

Vacation days plus additional days-off along the year.
Attractive salary.
Collective transport service in some sites.
On-site facilities (among others): free canteen, kindergarten, medical office.
Possibility to collaborate in different social and corporate social responsibility initiatives.
Excellent upskilling opportunities and great development prospects in a multicultural
environment.

Airbus is a leader in designing, manufacturing and delivering aerospace products, services and solutions
to customers on a worldwide scale, pioneering sustainable aerospace for a safe and united world.
With around 130,000 employees and as the largest aeronautics and space company in Europe and a
worldwide leader, Airbus is at the forefront of the aviation industry, connecting people and places via air
and space.
At Airbus, we support you to work, connect and collaborate more easily and flexibly. Wherever possible,
we foster flexible working arrangements to stimulate innovative thinking

If you want to conquer the world, we’re excited to welcome you onboard!

Job responsibilities

Training Administrative Management:

Coordinate and manage administrative processes related to training grants, ensuring compliance with deadlines and legal requirements.
Maintain accurate records and documentation of training activities.
Prepare and submit the necessary documentation for training grants, ensuring accuracy to avoid delays or rejections.

Support to the Learning & Development Manager:

Assist in the planning, organization, and execution of training and development programs and projects.
Collaborate in the creation and improvement of training materials, including presentations and supporting documents.

Support to the HR Department:

Provide administrative and coordination support for various HR activities and projects.
Manage calendars, organize meetings, and prepare materials as needed.

Monitoring and Analysis:

Track training participation and key performance indicators.
Use Excel to monitor data, create basic reports, and apply formulas such as VLOOKUP and data cross-references.

Profile

Microsoft Office Proficiency:
Excel: Advanced level, able to track data, apply basic formulas (VLOOKUP, data cross-referencing), and generate simple reports.
PowerPoint: Skilled in creating and improving professional presentations.


Languages: Witten and verbal fluency in both English and Spanish.


Knowledge of Training Grant Processes: Familiarity with regulations and procedures for managing subsidized training (highly valued).


Administrative Management: Proven experience in administrative and coordination tasks.


Proactivity and Persistence: Ability to anticipate needs, take initiative, and follow through on tasks, even in the face of obstacles or delays. Resilience when obtaining information on time is challenging.


Detail-Oriented and Methodical: High attention to accuracy and rigor in executing administrative and management tasks, especially in critical processes like training grants.


Interpersonal Skills: Ability to build strong professional relationships, be approachable, collaborative, and supportive, fostering teamwork to achieve common goals.


Autonomy: Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and make informed decisions within their area of responsibility.


Supportive Attitude: Willingness to assist other team members and the organization.


Required Experience:

Prior experience in administrative roles, preferably in Human Resources or Learning & Development.

Demonstrable experience in document management and process tracking with strong attention to detail.

Additional information

LVMH Beauty Iberia believes in every person’s unique talent and celebrates all singularities. Therefore, we foster diversity and inclusion from our recruitment process and are committed to promote equal opportunities to create an integrative working environment. As part of this commitment, LVMH Beauty Iberia values candidates who hold a certificate of disability equal to or greater than 33%.

Crafting Dreams Starts With Yours

At LVMH, people make the difference in the art of crafting dreams.
Our people fuel our dynamic, entrepreneurial culture. We value collective ambitions, encouraging our talents to push boundaries and champion a curious, audacious state of mind. Our commitment to excellence is reflected in nurturing every individual with a growth mindset and development opportunities, consistently empowering them to reach their full potential. We are actively committed to positive impact through an inclusive environment that supports and gives back to our talented community.
Join us at LVMH, where your talent is at the heart of our collective successes.

Recruitment & Talent

Summary

The Associate Director / Director of Human Resources will act as a strategic partner to commercial and functional leaders across a designated cluster of countries within the EMEA region.

The ideal candidate may be based in Italy or Spain.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

This role is pivotal in enabling the business to achieve its priorities by proactively designing and executing HR strategies aligned with organizational goals. The successful candidate will balance strategic leadership with hands-on operational delivery, ensuring HR practices are simplified, standardized, and compliant across global, regional, and local contexts

Strategic HR Leadership

Translate business priorities into actionable HR strategies that drive growth and performance.
Partner with cluster leadership to align workforce planning, talent development, and organizational design.

Operational & Tactical Delivery

Lead a team of 3–5 HR professionals, while leveraging Centers of Excellence (COEs) and cross-functional networks.
Roll up sleeves to manage tactical HR operations when needed, ensuring seamless execution.

Global–Regional–Local Integration

Simplify and standardize HR processes, balancing global frameworks with regional relevance and local compliance.
Ensure adherence to EU employment law and deep compliance with at least one country’s legal framework.

Change & Transformation

Sponsor and lead HR projects including M&A integration, spin-offs, restructuring, and talent mobility initiatives.
Drive organizational agility and resilience through proactive HR interventions.

Leadership Enablement

Act as a coach and trusted advisor to leaders and managers, enhancing their capability to lead teams effectively.
Serve as a brand ambassador, reinforcing company values and culture through HR practices.

Financial & Resource Management

Manage HR budgets of approximately $1M USD, ensuring efficient allocation of resources.
Oversee vendor relationships and optimize HR service delivery.

Culture & Engagement

Champion diversity, equity, and inclusion across the cluster.
Drive employee engagement through targeted initiatives and feedback mechanisms.

Requirements

Strong business acumen with ability to align HR strategy to commercial priorities.
Agile HR mindset with experience in driving organizational adaptability.
Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills across functions and geographies.
Hands-on approach with willingness to balance strategic initiatives and operational delivery.
High integrity, discretion, and ability to act as a trusted advisor to senior leadership.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (Master’s preferred).
Professional HR certification (SHRM, CIPD, or equivalent).
Associate Director level: 8+ years of progressive HR experience with exposure to cluster/regional responsibilities.
Director level: 12+ years of HR leadership experience in complex, multinational organizations with significant revenue responsibility.
Proven track record in M&A, restructuring, and organizational transformation.
Familiarity with EU employment law; deep expertise in at least one country’s employment legislation and practices.
Fluency in English plus at least one language spoken within the supported cluster (Italian/Spanish/Portuguese).

Description:

A MagmaCultura estem compromesos amb la cultura. Som un referent en el desenvolupament i gestió de projectes culturals, educatius i turístics. La nostra passió és promoure el valor de la cultura i fer-la accessible a les persones. El nostre motor principal és la professionalitat, el talent i el compromís del nostre equip expert en gestió cultural.

Un dels principals valors de MagmaCultura consisteix en la creació d'un lloc de treball en el qual totes les persones se sentin respectades, representades i incloses en un entorn segur en el qual puguin desenvolupar i impulsar la seva carrera professional amb èxit.

Per això mateix, com a institució compromesa amb la cultura i la diversitat, vetllem per la promoció d'un ambient de treball en el qual destaqui la igualtat de gènere i el respecte per motius de raça, ètnia, sexe, orientació sexual i/o diversitat funcional.

Volem incorporar un/a Tècnic/a laboral (RRHH) que acompanyi, assessori i gestioni els processos laborals dels nostres equips.

Imagina't com serà el teu dia a dia:

Acompanyar i assessorar els equips en matèria laboral.
Gestionar els contractes i tràmits vinculats a la relació laboral (altes, baixes, modificacions, variacions de jornada i annexos).
Fer un seguiment d'absències, vacances i incapacitats, assegurant una gestió ordenada.
Interpretar i aplicar els convenis col·lectius vigents i segons les seves actualitzacions.
Garantir la correcta gestió documental del personal.
Revisar les nòmines al costat de els/as Responsables de cada servei.
Tramitar sancions i comunicacions disciplinàries quan sigui necessari, acompanyant als equips.
Realitzar càlcul de quitances i indemnitzacions.

Què t'oferim:

Formar part d'un projecte cultural amb impacte real en les persones i el patrimoni.
Integrar-te a un equip pròxim on la col·laboració, l'aprenentatge i la bona energia són la base de tot el que fem.
Un entorn on el teu coneixement i les teves idees són valorades i tenen impacte real.
Un espai on tu ets el protagonista del teu desenvolupament: t'acompanyem, et formem i et donem oportunitats reals de creixement professional.

La teva proposta de contractació inclou:

Jornada laboral: 37,5 hores setmanals.
Dies i horaris de treball: de dilluns a dijous entrada flexible entre les 08.00 h i les 09.00 h, sortida entre les 17.00 h i les 18.00 h.; divendres jornada intensiva. Juliol i agost, jornada intensiva.
Tipus de contracte: Indefinit.
Lloc de treball: Barcelona.

Requirements:

Ser una persona organitzada, amb capacitat per a transmetre informació complexa, clara i pròxima.
Que gaudeixi treballant de manera col·laborativa, aportant idees i solucions.
Formació en Recursos Humans, Relacions Laborals, Dret Laboral, Administració d'Empreses o equivalent.
Posseir coneixements sòlids en normativa laboral vigent i la seva correcta aplicació.
Domini del paquet Office, especialment Excel.
Idiomes: català i castellà natiu/bilingüe i anglès avançat. Altres idiomes seran benvinguts i valorats.
Experiència prèvia en gestió laboral, nòmines o administració de personal.
El més important: ser una persona amb entusiasme i amb capacitat de fer viure experiències úniques a través de l’art i la cultura.

En Asteasu, Gipuzkoa, se busca una persona con experiencia y pasión por los recursos humanos, específicamente en la gestión de nóminas. Esta posición es una oportunidad ideal para quienes disfrutan del área de asesoría y gestoría, ya que será fundamental para asegurar la correcta administración de las nóminas dentro de un entorno de trabajo dinámico y presencial.

La empresa está inmersa en un proceso de modernización, implementando un nuevo sistema de gestión de Recursos Humanos llamado Factorial y actualizando su proveedor de nóminas. Para acompañar esta transición, se necesita temporalmente a alguien que brinde apoyo en el traspaso de información, la realización de pruebas y en la digitalización general del departamento, garantizando así una adaptación fluida a los nuevos procesos.

Las responsabilidades incluirán el procesamiento preciso de nóminas, el cumplimiento de la normativa laboral y fiscal vigente, la atención a consultas de los empleados, y la colaboración en la gestión administrativa del personal. Se valorará la experiencia en asesoría o gestoría, así como habilidades organizativas, conocimiento de herramientas informáticas para nóminas y una actitud proactiva y orientada al detalle.

Fluidra is looking for an HR Data Analytics Trainee to join our HR team in our Sant Cugat. This is the ideal role for someone who enjoys diving into data hands‑on experience and looking for a strong foundation for future roles in HRBP, People Analytics, or HR Operations, this is a great opportunity.

WHAT YOU WILL CONTRIBUTE

We are looking for a proactive and analytical HR Data Analytics Intern to support both the data and strategic sides of the HR function. This hybrid role combines data gathering, analysis, and insight creation with exposure to HR Business Partnering activities.

You will support HRBPs by turning data from multiple HR and business systems into actionable insights that influence decisions related to workforce planning, overhead management, organizational design, and major transformation projects. You will also collaborate with HRBPs on people initiatives, employee experience work, and business partnering routines.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1. HR Data Analysis & Reporting

Collect, consolidate, and clean data from various HR and business systems (e.g., HRIS, Finance, Talent, Engagement platforms).

Build dashboards and recurring reports that track headcount, organizational changes, overtime, turnover, overheads, and talent indicators.

Support predictive and scenario analyses for transformation, restructuring, or workforce planning initiatives.

Ensure data quality and integrity across sources.


2. HRBP Support & People Strategy


Assist HRBPs with workforce planning, recruitment follow‑up, performance cycle preparations, and talent management activities.

Contribute to the preparation of business reviews, org updates, and people-related presentations for leadership.

Participate in meetings with HRBPs and business leaders to understand context and support decision‑making.


3. Insights & Recommendations


Translate data into meaningful insights for HRBPs, leaders, and cross‑functional partners.

Identify emerging trends in workforce, organizational, and financial data that could impact business decisions.

Support storytelling with data by creating compelling visuals, summaries, and recommendations.


4. Tools & Continuous Improvement

Use Excel, Power BI, or other visualization tools to improve data accessibility for HRBPs and leaders.

Help automate manual reports and standardize analytics processes.

Suggest enhancements to systems, dashboards, and workflows to improve efficiency.

WHAT WE SEEK


Degree in Business, HR, Data Analytics, Economics, Psychology, Engineering, or a related field. Finalized Degreed is a must for this type of internship labor contract. If you haven't finished your degree, you can apply for a scholarship through an agreement with the university.

Requirement to make an internship contract: not having previously done internships with contracts 420 and 520 (this can be seen by downloading the work history on the internet)

Strong proficiency in Excel; experience with Power BI or similar tools is an asset.

Comfortable analyzing large datasets and identifying patterns.

Basic understanding of HR processes (recruitment, performance, talent, organizational design) is preferred.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking abilities.

Excellent communication: capable of turning complex data into clear messages.

Detail‑oriented, organized, and able to handle multiple priorities.

Curious, collaborative, and eager to learn about both HR and analytics.

English and Spanish at proficiency level


WHAT WE OFFER

Dynamic and friendly work environment

Opportunities for professional growth and development in the leading company in it’s industry

1 year contract with possibilities to explore opportunities in HR

Hybrid work with 3 days at the office in Sant Cugat next to the train station


ABOUT FLUIDRA


Fluidra, a multinational group listed on the Spanish Stock Exchange, is the global leader in the pool and wellness industry. Founded in 1969, Fluidra has long-standing experience in developing innovative products and services in the global residential and commercial pool market. The company operates in over 45 countries, has over 7,000 employees and owns a portfolio of some of the industry’s most recognized and trusted brands: Polaris, Jandy, CMP, S.R. Smith, and Zodiac. We also sell products under the Cover‐Pools, iAquaLink, Grand Effects, Del and Nature2 names. With these combined resources we’re able accelerate innovation in critical areas like energy-efficiency, robotics and the Internet of Things.

Purpose is we turn water into a better world. We take our purpose to heart, and our employees embody these guiding principles in everything we do: passion for success, honesty & trust, customer collaboration, teamwork and inclusion, learn and adapt, excellence and innovation.

You may be just the right candidate for this role or another role in the organization. Fluidra is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Fluidra recruits, employs, trains, compensates and promotes regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, veteran status, or any legally protected characteristic

Bridgestone Americas, Inc. (BSAM), headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, and Bridgestone Europe, Middle East and Africa (BSEMEA), headquartered in Brussels, Belgium, operate collectively as a “Bridgestone West” strategic region. This region services the strategic business needs of teams across the Americas, Europe, Middle East and Africa. BSAM and BSEMEA are subsidiaries of Bridgestone Corporation, globally headquartered in Japan. Bridgestone and its subsidiaries develop, manufacture and market a wide range of Bridgestone, Firestone and associate brand products and solutions to address the needs of a broad range of customers and industries.

ABOUT THE ROLE

Purpose

At First Stop Southwest, part of Bridgestone’s retail network in EMEA, we’re building a high performing, people first organization that delivers superior service and sustainable growth.
We’re looking for an HR Specialist (Maternity Cover) to partner with our First Stop Southwest leadership and store network, driving the full people agenda—from workforce planning and talent development to engagement, performance, safety, employee relations and compliance.
You’ll be a visible, trusted advisor to leaders and teammates, translating business goals into practical HR solutions and championing our LEAD leadership framework to empower teams, advance results, develop connections, and lead the future.


Responsabilities
I/ Change Management, Culture & Engagement
You’ll play a key role in shaping and strengthening our culture.
Partner with leaders to craft clear, inspiring communication that supports change and drives alignment.
Work hand in hand with our Talent Acquisition CoE to attract top external talent, ensuring hiring practices reflect our organisational needs and strengthen our partnership with external recruitment providers.
Champion a culture that brings our vision to life—embedding Bridgestone’s Essence and Behaviours in everything we do.


II/ Performance, Talent & Development
You’ll support leaders in building high performing and future ready teams.
Guide managers in setting meaningful individual and team objectives that link directly to business strategy.
Coach leaders on differentiating performance, addressing gaps, and identifying high potential talent.
Ensure training, development, and career growth initiatives align with organisational priorities.
Actively contribute to talent identification, succession planning, and capability building aligned with regional strategy.


III/ Labour Relations & Compliance
Manage local works councils.
Negotiate and implement changes in working conditions (departmental structure or staff allocation, working hours, transfers, etc.).
Coordinate, with external labour consultants, any issues related to this field.
Manage cases raised through the Speak Up channel with objectivity and discretion.
Conduct thorough investigations, including relevant interviews, and ensure appropriate follow up actions.


IV/ Compensation & Benefits
You’ll support fair and competitive rewards that attract and retain talent.
Partner with managers to develop compensation proposals for hires and promotions within internal guidelines.
Advise leaders on Bridgestone’s total rewards philosophy—supporting performance differentiation, recognition, and the retention of key talent.


V/ Other Responsibilities
You’ll support operational excellence across HR.
Promote the effective use of HRIS tools (SuccessFactors, GoBeyond, GoFluent, etc.) to ensure data accuracy and process compliance.
Ensure policies, procedures, and documentation meet national legal requirements, and that employees receive the right compliance training.
Monitor HR performance metrics and drive continuous improvement in HR service delivery.

Leadership and influencing skills
Communication excellence
Stakeholder Management and collaboration
Labour Relations and negotiations
Change Management and culture building
Operational HR excellence
Compensation and rewards literacy

Proficiency in Office
Knowledge of Success Factors is an advantage
High level of English (written and spoken) is a must (French is an advantage)
Ability to prioritise and multitask
Experience working in global and matrix environments

Availability to travel

RISK is an integrated and independent control function of the BNP Paribas Group. It is the second line of defense on the risk management activities of the Group which are under its direct responsibilities, including credit and counterparty risk, market risk, funding and liquidity risk, interest rate and foreign exchange risks in the banking book, insurance risk, operational risk, and environmental and social risks.

Global Payroll and Benefits Manager

ES EL MOMENTO

Santander ( www.santander.com ) está evolucionando de ser una marca global de alto impacto a una organización impulsada por la tecnología , y las personas están en el centro de este camino. Juntos, estamos liderando una transformación centrada en el cliente que valora el pensamiento disruptivo , la valentía de desafiar lo posible y la capacidad de innovar .

Esto es más que un cambio estratégico: es una oportunidad para crecer, aprender y generar un impacto real .

Nuestra misión es contribuir a que más personas y empresas prosperen . Adoptamos una sólida cultura de riesgos y esperamos que todos nuestros equipos asuman un enfoque proactivo y responsable en la gestión del riesgo.

EL IMPACTO QUE GENERARÁS

P&C Global Delivery está buscando un/a Global Payroll and Be nefits Manager con base en Boadilla.

Tu misión será liderar y optimizar la estrategia global de retribución y beneficios para todos los empleados activos y no activos con vinculación económica, asegurando competitividad externa, equidad interna, eficiencia operativa y cumplimiento legal en los más de 30 países en los que opera la compañía

Estamos redefiniendo nuestra forma de trabajar a través de la innovación, la tecnología de última generación, la colaboración y la libertad de explorar nuevas ideas.

En este puesto, tus principales responsabilidades incluirán:

Implementar y liderar la estrategia global/local (geografía española) de retribución total (fija, variable, incentivos a largo plazo, acciones) alineada con los objetivos corporativos.
Gobernar y optimizar los procesos globales/locales (geografía española) de nómina, seguridad social y gestión retributiva, garantizando eficiencia, control y cumplimiento regulatorio.
Diseñar y coordinar programas globales de beneficios flexibles, seguros, planes de pensiones, bienestar y otros elementos no monetarios, adaptados al contexto local y global.
Gestionar la política y procesos locales de retribución y beneficios para colectivos no activos con vínculo laboral (excedencias, prejubilaciones, jubilaciones, colectivos protegidos, etc.).
Liderar la definición de paquetes retributivos para el talento clave, incluyendo expatriados, perfiles críticos y altos directivos.
Supervisar auditorías internas y externas e Inspecciones en materia retributiva y de beneficios.
Coordinar con áreas de Payroll, Finanzas, Legal, Compliance y Relaciones Laborales Asesoría Fiscal, Laboral y Jurídico para garantizar una ejecución integrada y sinérgica.
Impulsar la automatización, digitalización y escalabilidad de los procesos de compensación y beneficios.
Asegurar la calidad y consistencia de la información retributiva para reportes regulatorios, de sostenibilidad (ESG), y de igualdad retributiva.

Nuestra gente es nuestra mayor fortaleza. Cada persona contribuye con perspectivas únicas que nos hacen más fuertes como equipo y como organización. Valoramos quiénes son y potenciamos lo que aportan.

VALORAMOS TU IMPACTO

Tu contribución importa, y la reconocemos. En Santander puedes esperar un paquete retributivo justo y competitivo que refleje tu impacto y el valor que entregarás. Toma un momento para entender a lo que nos referimos:

Crecimiento sin límites. Impulsamos a nuestros equipos con oportunidades globales y amplias trayectorias de carrera para que puedan llegar más lejos.

Y esto es solo el comienzo… ¡te contaremos mucho más cuando te unas!

Estamos aquí para mantener tu motivación , ayudarte a alcanzar tus metas y celebrar tus logros en cada paso del camino.

En MagmaCultura estamos comprometidos con la cultura. Somos un referente en el desarrollo y la gestión de proyectos culturales, educativos y turísticos. Nuestra pasión es promover el valor de la cultura y hacerla accesible a las personas. Nuestro principal motor es la profesionalidad, el talento y el compromiso de nuestro equipo experto en gestión cultural.

Uno de los principales valores de MagmaCultura consiste en la creación de un puesto de trabajo en el que todas las personas se sientan respetadas, representadas e incluidas en un entorno seguro en el que puedan desarrollar e impulsar su carrera profesional con éxito.

Por eso mismo, como institución comprometida con la cultura y la diversidad, velamos por la promoción de un ambiente de trabajo en el que destaque la igualdad de género y el respeto por motivos de raza, etnia, sexo, orientación sexual y/o diversidad funcional.

Asesorar y acompañar a Responsables y equipos en cuestiones laborales.
Gestionar contratos, altas, bajas, modificaciones y variaciones de jornada.
Realizar seguimiento de ausencias, vacaciones e incapacidades, asegurando una gestión ordenada.
Interpretar y aplicar convenios colectivos vigentes.
Revisar nóminas junto a Responsables de servicios.
Calcular finiquitos e indemnizaciones.
Gestionar comunicaciones disciplinarias con criterio y acompañamiento.
Garantizar una gestión documental rigurosa y ordenada.

Qué te ofrecemos:

Formar parte de un proyecto cultural con impacto real.
Un equipo cercano donde la colaboración y el aprendizaje son parte del día a día.
Un entorno donde tu criterio profesional cuenta, tus ideas suman y tu aportación marca la diferencia.

Access & Belonging (Equal Opportunity)

We hire for impact and potential, not pedigree.
We welcome applications from people with non-linear careers, career breaks, caregiving gaps, and those changing fields.

No discrimination on the basis of age, disability, gender identity/expression, marital or family status, pregnancy, neurodivergence, race/ethnicity, religion/belief, gender, sexual orientation, or any other protected ground.

Assessment fairness:
We anchor on evidence of outcomes (what you shipped, moved, or influenced).
We actively de-bias by using structured rubrics, multiple assessors, and blind screening most of the time (we won’t know your name, gender, or personal info until the interview stage).

Application Clarity

No cover letter required.
Apply with your CV in English.
You may be asked a few short, relevant questions.

Total candidate time investment: ~3 hours end-to-end.

Hiring Stages (What to Expect, Why & How Long)
1. Applications Screen — Signal check vs must-haves

Done by People + Hiring Manager.
You’ll hear from us within 8 business days.

2. Role-Fit Questionnaire (async)
Purpose: capture signals your CV can’t (languages, tools, scenario judgement) and calibrate seniority.
Format: a few questions.

3. Quick Interview (Intro Call) - Mutual fit & context • 20 min
With Talent Partner. Covers role scope, salary, location, and timeline.

4. Hiring Manager Interview - Deep dive into your work • 30 min
Structured around outcomes, decisions, and collaboration.

5. Challenge Role Play - Let's peak into this role's challenges • 60 min
Exercises that represent potential challenges this role would have and how you'd approach them.

6. People Interview - Allow us to know you better! • 30 min
With People. Stress-free virtual coffee, focused on getting to know you as a person. We talk about culture, beliefs, and purpose.

7. Final Conversation (CEO) — Values, strategy, and your growth • 30 min

Optional: References (2–3 people who’ve seen your recent work) - async.

AI & Hiring Tools Transparency

We use a few tools to reduce bias and improve documentation, not to make hiring decisions.

Teamtailor anonymisation: profiles are reviewed without relying on names/personal identifiers.

Meeting recorder (e.g., Talka.ai): may be used to capture interviews so we can focus on the conversation.

ChatGPT: may be used to turn interview notes/transcripts into clear, structured summaries.

Important: every application is reviewed by a human, and no decision or rejection is made by AI. If recording is used, we’ll be transparent and (where required) ask for consent.

️ Speed & Communication

Decision: within 4 weeks of your application.

Updates: weekly if the process runs longer.

Scheduling: interviews between 10:00–16:00 CET (flexible across Europe).

Feedback: from the Case stage onwards, you’ll always receive written or verbal feedback - what went well, and what to strengthen next time.

Work Where It’s Best for You

Work Anywhere means you can work in an office or at home on any given day. It’s about getting the work done in the way and place that works best for each person. This applies across all locations and departments.

Work Anywhere does not mean work at any time. We have predictable core hours where employees are generally available for meetings and collaboration. Employees are focused and available during core hours.

We invest in our offices to make them places where our employees like to go. If you work in the office three or more days a week, you will have a dedicated office workspace. Our offices function as hubs to draw people in, create social bonds, and where random connections and mixing of ideas happen. We’re investing more in offices, culture, and offsite meetings, not less.

Product teams are organized in regional product hubs for optimal collaboration and live within a time zone of their hub. Our current product hubs are located in Pleasanton, Columbus, Boston, Kansas City, New York City, Raleigh, and Toronto. We create opportunities for teams to get together in person regularly.

Customer-facing roles, such as Sales and Professional Services, live near and/or travel to their customers.

When an employee moves within a country it does not cause a change in salary. Where you live impacts you and your family. Not knowing if your compensation will change if you move can cause stress and uncertainty for everyone. We wanted to eliminate that.

Work at Veeva. Work where it’s best for you.

Empresa industrial de entorno productivo busca incorporar un/a Responsable de Recursos Humanos para liderar y desarrollar la función de RRHH en una de sus unidades operativas, en coordinación con las directrices corporativas del grupo. La posición tiene un marcado enfoque estratégico y operativo, actuando como socio clave del negocio y acompañando a la organización en un contexto de evolución y mejora continua de los procesos de personas.

Misión

Impulsar una gestión integral de Recursos Humanos alineada con los objetivos del negocio, asegurando la correcta implantación de políticas, procesos y herramientas de RRHH, y actuando como referente en materia laboral, organizativa y de desarrollo del talento dentro del entorno industrial. Sus principales responsabilidades serán:

Partner del negocio: colaborar estrechamente con la dirección de la unidad para apoyar la toma de decisiones en materia de personas, estructura organizativa y planificación de recursos.

Gestión del cambio y desarrollo organizativo: participar en procesos de evolución organizativa, dimensionamiento de plantillas y mejora del clima laboral.

Talento y desarrollo: definir e implantar iniciativas de atracción, desarrollo, fidelización y compromiso del talento, adaptadas a las necesidades del entorno productivo.

Relaciones laborales: actuar como interlocutor/a en materia laboral, garantizando el cumplimiento normativo y gestionando las relaciones con los diferentes agentes sociales.

Gestión de datos y reporting: analizar indicadores de RRHH y elaborar reportes que faciliten una gestión basada en datos y orientada a resultados.

Coordinación del área de RRHH: organizar y supervisar la actividad del área, asegurando un nivel de servicio adecuado y alineado con las políticas corporativas, así como la participación en proyectos transversales del grupo.

Requisitos

Formación: Titulación universitaria en Derecho, ADE, Psicología, Relaciones Laborales o similar. Se valorará formación de posgrado en Recursos Humanos o Dirección de Empresas.

Experiencia: trayectoria consolidada en posiciones de responsabilidad en RRHH, preferiblemente en entornos industriales o productivos.

Conocimientos: legislación laboral, gestión del talento, procesos de RRHH y herramientas de gestión de personas.

Idiomas: nivel B2 de inglés y deseable manejo de euskera

Competencias clave: liderazgo, visión estratégica, orientación a resultados, capacidad de negociación, toma de decisiones, comunicación efectiva, adaptabilidad al cambio y trabajo en equipo.

Se ofrece

Incorporación a un proyecto sólido y en evolución, con un rol clave dentro de la organización y amplias posibilidades de desarrollo profesional. Condiciones económicas acordes a la responsabilidad del puesto y a la experiencia aportada.

Payroll & Personnel Administration (Regional Scope):

The selected candidate will be responsible for payroll and personnel administration in Spain, Turkey, Germany and Italy.

Ensure correct taxation and social security contributions.

Prepare employment documentation (promotions, salary changes, terminations, etc.).

Stay updated on labor, tax and social security regulations in the assigned countries

Ensure data accuracy and system integrity.

Prepare regular reports on headcount, personnel costs and other HR KPIs.

Support internal audits and reporting requirements.

Support internal and external payroll and HR audits.

Ensure compliance with corporate and legal requirements in all assigned countries.

Collaborate in the preparation of the annual Sustainability Report and the Non-Financial Information Statement (EINF).

Provide HR data and analysis required for corporate and regulatory reporting.

Bachelor’s degree in Labour Relations, Business Administration, Economics or similar fields related to HR and employment matters.

This role will act as a replacement within the team and will play a key part in managing end-to-end hiring processes, particularly supporting roles requiring English and Portuguese language capabilities.

You will partner closely with hiring managers and HR stakeholders across different business units, ensuring efficient, high-quality hiring aligned with business priorities. This role is ideal for a recruitment professional who is comfortable working in an international environment, managing multiple requisitions simultaneously and adapting to different markets and hiring needs.

Manage the full recruitment lifecycle, from intake to offer stage, for roles based primarily in Iberia and across EMEA.
Partner with hiring managers to define role requirements, hiring strategy, and realistic market expectations.
Proactively collaborate with our Coordinators team to source candidates using LinkedIn, job boards, internal talent pools, and referrals, with a focus on profiles requiring English and Portuguese.
Conduct structured candidate screenings and interviews to assess skills, motivation, and cultural fit.
Coordinate interview processes and ensure a smooth, professional candidate experience.
Collaborate with HR, coordinators, and onboarding teams to ensure compliant and timely hiring.
Maintain accurate recruitment data and reporting within the ATS.
Contribute to continuous improvement of recruitment processes, sourcing strategies, and stakeholder engagement.

Proven experience in Talent Acquisition or Recruitment, ideally within a multinational or EMEA-focused environment.
Experience recruiting across multiple roles and functions (IT, sales, corporate, or operational roles is a plus).
Fluency in English and Portuguese (written and spoken) is required; Spanish is a strong plus.
Strong sourcing skills and hands-on experience with LinkedIn Recruiter and ATS platforms.
Strong stakeholder management skills with the confidence to advise and challenge hiring managers when needed.
Organized, resilient, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, international environment.
Based in or willing to work from Barcelona.

Descripción del empleo


Position Summary

The Human Resource Manager will lead and direct the routine functions of the Human Resources (HR) department including onboarding staff, administering pay, benefits, and leave, and enforcing company policies and practices at local Canada facility.

Essential Duties and Responsibilities

The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following:

Supervisory Responsibilities:

Establishing and executing department priorities to ensure 5-10 supervisees follow organizational policy and procedures with the primary focus of human resources
Monitoring employee productivity to provide positive feedback, verbal coaching, written disciplinary actions, as well as cyclical performance evaluations
Set goals for department performance as it pertains to individual employee performance and succession planning
Onboard and train new staff in the HR department.

Duties/Responsibilities:

The essential functions include, but are not limited to the following:

Oversees the daily workflow of the HR department.
Assist discipline and termination of employees at the instruction of the HR Director/Executives in accordance with company policies
Partners with the leadership team to understand and execute the organization’s human resource and talent strategy developed by the HR Director particularly as it relates to current and future talent needs, recruiting, retention, and succession planning.
Provides support and guidance to HR generalists, management, and other staff when complex, specialized, and sensitive questions and issues arise; may be required to administer and execute routine tasks in delicate circumstances such as providing reasonable accommodations, investigating allegations of wrongdoing, and terminations at the direction of the HR Director.
Implement learning and development programs and initiatives created by the HR Director that provide internal development opportunities for employees.
Assist with employee disciplinary meetings, terminations, and investigations.
Assist with maintaining compliance with federal, provincial, state, and local employment laws and regulations, and recommended best practices; reviews policies and practices to maintain compliance.
Maintains knowledge of trends, best practices, regulatory changes, and new technologies in human resources, talent management, and employment law.
Performs other duties as assigned.

About The Role
The Manager, Workday Compensation and Performance role is responsible for the product management of the Workday Compensation, Talent, and Advanced Compensation Management modules. This position will initially focus on driving our Workday implementation to success, then transition to continuous support and optimization of Workday. The role combines strategic vision and technical expertise to drive operational excellence through effective collaboration with stakeholders and optimal process design.
The Ideal Candidate Will Have
A strong background in compensation strategy and administration with deep understanding of salary structures, pay practices, and total rewards frameworks
A commitment to continuous improvement and focus on delivering best in class user experiences in the moments that matter
Proven experience anticipating, defining, and recommending solutions to complex compensation and HR business problems related to pay equity, market positioning, and performance management
Strong project and change management skills with a proven ability to drive alignment among stakeholders across cross functional teams, particularly during compensation cycle planning and execution
Deep analytical capabilities with attention to detail in managing compensation data integrity, conducting pay equity analysis, and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements

What You'll Do
Support the successful implementation of Workday Compensation and Job Architecture as well as Talent and Advanced Compensation modules by coordinating project activities, tracking deliverables, and ensuring project milestones are met
Act as functional subject matter expert for Workday Compensation, Talent and Advanced Compensation modules, partnering with cross-functional teams including HR leadership, HRIS, HR Centers of Excellence (CoEs), Finance, Payroll, and local market HR teams to provide guidance and consultation on best practices and optimization strategy
Configure and maintain Workday business processes and compensation plans including merit cycles, bonus planning, stock plans, salary structures, grade profiles, job profiles, job families, and eligibility rules

Responsible for identifying and implementing enhancements that will improve user experience, drive adoption, and maintain overall system hygiene and health

Participate in the development and execution of the strategic roadmap for our Workday ecosystem, including module expansions, feature adoption, and integration strategy
Monitor Workday product updates and releases, testing new features, documenting changes, and implementing enhancements to optimize system efficiency
Complete continuing education to increase domain and product knowledge
Drive process optimization and automation initiatives across the HR technology landscape
Continuously analyze and evaluate current state solutions and advise on best practices to deliver best in class configurations
Collaborate with stakeholders to influence and support HR technology and business process initiatives
Act as subject matter expert for end-users and HR business partners, resolving system issues, access problems, and process flaws related to compensation and performance modules
Manage direct reports, as needed

How You'll Grow

We are committed to investing and supporting you in your professional development and we have developed a range of programs focused on fostering leadership, growth and development opportunities. We aim to promote continuous learning and individual skills development through on-the-job learning, self-guided professional development courses and certifications. As you progress through your career at FTI Consulting, we offer tailored programs for critical professional milestones to ensure you are prepared and empowered to take on your next role.

The Opportunity

Hitachi Energy is seeking an experienced, senior Human Resources Business Partner as HR leader for our High Voltage Business Unit Europe with more than 3000 employees across 26 countries.

As a core member of the High Voltage Business Unit leadership team in Europe, you are accountable for the overall HR contribution and value-add to the business. In your role as HR Business Partner Europe (Director level), you translate business goals into HR priorities and ensure their effective implementation in partnership with our Centers of Expertise (CoEs) and HR Operations. You will also lead a dedicated, international team of HR Business Partners.

In this role, you partner with business leaders to define and implement the people strategy, develop and drive talent outcomes, and support business goals and initiatives by ensuring the right people capabilities and organizational effectiveness are in place. You counsel and coach business leaders, advise on appropriate solutions, and represent the business in its interactions with the CoEs and HR Operations to ensure alignment and the delivery of effective HR support. The location for this role is flexible within Europe and will be determined in subsequent stages.

How you will make an impact

Strategy leadership: Set and implement people strategy in line with global business unit and global HR function strategy.
Capability & Workforce Planning: Ensure people and organizational capabilities required in the mid-long term are understood and actioned. Ensure that the business positions in the short term are adequately staffed to meet or exceed the annual business targets. Interrogate HR Analytics data to understand key organizational dynamics, trends and issues and define and execute appropriate actions.
Organizational Design & Development: Implement the operating model and optimize organization structures and principles in line with group and global rules and standards, including the Hitachi Energy Job Structure.
Performance Management: Build a high-performance culture in the organization; Implement group and global business performance standards, KPIs, and targets to enhance business performance; Recognize and reward high performance in individuals and teams.
Talent Management: in close collaboration with the Talent Management CoE, set and implement strategies and actions to ensure a healthy and sustainable talent structure in the organization, including recruitment, identification and succession planning on business-critical positions, diversity and inclusion.

The Role of Assistant Director of People and Culture

The Four Seasons Hotel Madrid is looking for an Assistant Director of People and Culture to join our team.

The Assistant Director of People and Culture works closely alongside the Director of People and Culture and oversees the day to day running of the People and Culture function. You will create an environment that focuses on fairness, inclusion, and is service centric.

The Assistant Director of People + Culture will oversee the growth and development of the People + Culture team, oversee activities including employee relations, workforce planning, coaching and development of both leaders and employees, and culture building.

People and Culture functions:

Assess the needs in areas such as performance management, employee relations, compensation, career development and leadership development and drive the appropriate solutions in these areas.
Act as liaison between employee and management to answer questions or concerns regarding company policies, practices and regulations.
Identify, analyze and initiate appropriate actions to resolve employee concerns.
Manage the financial issues for the People and Culture budget including benefits, labor & salary reports and plan cost effective events within the People and Culture budget guidelines.
Work closely with Finance regarding labor meeting, employee attestations, wage and hour training.
Organize and supervise all employee programs - recognition celebrations, community outreach, internal meetings, etc.
Respond to employee claims, Workers’ Compensation, Unemployment and Wage & Hour.
Act as liaison between employee and management to answer questions or concerns regarding company policies, practices and regulations.
Work harmoniously and professionally with co-workers and supervisors while maintaining the confidentiality of People and Culture.
Responsible for hiring process, including contracting and onboarding.
Coordinates all aspects of recruitment including job postings, interviews and job offers, tracking applicants and ensuring follow up.
Understanding the importance of Interviewing and providing guidance and counsel to our managers throughout the process.
Own recruitment and find innovative ways to find staff. Review and analyze recruitment trends: Turnover, Resort Promotions, local and industry information.
Create and develop opportunities to attract, develop and retain people to achieve their fullest potential.
Develop own team and supports L&D Manager in her global role.
Our ideal Assistant Director of People and Culture candidate will have:


Previous experience in similar position.
Excellent writte and verbal skills. High level of English and a minimum average level of Spanish is required.
Exposure to labor law and employment equity regulations.
Effective HR administration and people management skills.
Full understanding of HR functions and best practices.
Works well under pressure and meets tight deadlines.
Highly computer literate with capability in email, MS Office and related business and communication tools.
Fantastic organizational and time management skills.
Strong decision-making and problem-solving skills.
Attention to detail.
Must have the legal right to work in Spain.

Brief Call: Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!

Case study: Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
We look forward to meeting you!

The role plays a key part in supporting HR transformation initiatives and embedding scalable, data-driven People Services across Europe.

People Services & Case Management

Lead and manage the European People Services Team, ensuring effective handling of employee and manager queries through the case management system in line with agreed SLAs and quality standards.
Define service levels and performance metrics for People Services processes; regularly review performance, identify gaps and implement corrective actions where SLAs are not met.

Problem Solving & Operational Excellence

Apply structured problem-solving approaches to resolve complex or recurring issues, plan communications, training, support for recurring requests or increase in help requests on specific topics.
Work closely with CoEs, People Partners and other People Services Managers to align processes, share best practices and resolve cross-regional challenges.
Partner with People Centres of Excellence and Senior People Business Partners to resolve complex queries or cases that fall outside standard policy.
Provide structured feedback to COEs and Senior People Business Partners on recurring policy questions, trends or areas requiring clarification.
Align with Senior People Business Partners on transition activities from country, cluster or studio-based support models into the People Services Teams.
Collaborate closely with HRIS on system troubleshooting, access management and escalations.

Insights, Reporting and Data Integrity

Provide regular insights and reporting on case volumes, trends and service performance to Regional People Directors and Senior People Business Partners.
Ensure data accuracy and integrity through regular reviews of reports and system data.

Continuous Improvement

Identify opportunities to improve service delivery, processes and employee experience across People Services and harmonize them globally, when possible.
Support and drive continuous improvement initiatives in collaboration with HRIS, COEs and People Business Partners.
Actively promote the adoption of employee and manager self-service tools and portals.

HR Systems & Configuration Support

Partner with HRIS to support the rollout and integration of new Workday modules and system enhancements
Initiate and support M&A-related requests for new studio set-ups, ensuring correct organisational structures and access are established.

5–10 years of professional experience in HR with solid exposure to HR Administration.

Strong problem-solving and analytical skills, with experience using HR data and metrics to identify root causes and drive effective solutions.
Proactive mindset, with the ability to anticipate change, identify opportunities and take ownership.

Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 65+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 10,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About the role

At Revolut, Operations means problem-solving at scale. Our team tackles the company’s toughest challenges with speed, precision, and creativity. They design systems that unlock efficiency and power global growth.

We’re looking for a Strategy & Operations Manager to support the delivery of our People strategy as we continue to scale across Europe. You’ll partner with the Head of HR in Europe to manage and deliver key HR processes for our growing organisation.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What you’ll be doing

Liaising with teams, such as Legal, Compliance, Risk, and Finance on analysing and defining key HR data
Running performance and talent cycles (performance review cycles, growth of talent, compensation checks, employee surveys)
Performing data analytics and reporting to deliver data and reports to key internal and external stakeholders, including governance bodies and regulators
Managing outsourced service providers, monitoring their performance, and ensuring scope of services are in line with requirements
Managing a variety of projects, including key change initiatives, new entity set-up, regulatory-related updates, etc.
Supporting the People function on risk management, quality standards, mapping and documenting processes

What you'll need

At least a 2:1 degree from a top university
4+ years of experience in a fast-paced environment
Project management skills to effectively plan, coordinate, and deliver people, risk, and reporting initiatives on time and with clear ownership and milestones
Hands-on coding experience with SQL and Python
The ability to meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment
Excellent analytical and organisational skills with impeccable attention to detail
To be comfortable with a degree of ambiguity that requires analysis and action
A solid track record of taking ownership and leading complex projects end to end
A magic skill to break complex problems into smaller ones and to enjoy working with data
Natural curiosity and a will to make an impact

Nice to have

HR or consulting experience

Compensation range

Poland: PLN18,000 - PLN20,000 gross monthly*
Lithuania: €4,100 - €4,500 gross monthly*
Other locations: Compensation will be discussed during the interview process
Final compensation will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, skills, and previous experience

Why join us
Get ready to Own service excellence as an HR Service Partner at Nutreco, an SHV company . As global leaders in animal nutrition and aquafeed, our shared purpose of Feeding the Future is making a positive global impact for generations to come. Join us and place employee experience at its heart as you Own your ideas, Own your direction and Own your career success.

What you’ll do
You’ll focus on delivering outstanding HR support across multiple countries, ensuring smooth employee lifecycle management and driving process improvements. Acting as a trusted point of contact, you’ll guide employees and managers through systems and policies, resolve queries on compensation, benefits, and leave, and ensure accurate data handling in global HR processes.
You’ll also play a key role in harmonizing and digitalizing HR operations, contributing ideas and participating in initiatives that make processes simpler and more efficient for our employees and teams.

Who we’re looking for
We’re looking for someone who is proactive, organized, and ready to Own this role. Someone with strong communication skills, customer focus, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in an international environment.
You’ll also need:

Bachelor’s degree and at least 2 years of experience in HR, administration, or customer service in a global setting
Fluent in English with good Spanish; proficiency in German, Dutch, or Norwegian is a plus.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Outlook, Word, Excel)
Experience with Workday or other HR systems (preferred)
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail

What you’ll receive
As part of Nutreco, you’ll be a member of the SHV family, a diverse group of companies with global footprint. Join us and you’ll gain access to a wide network of expertise and global career paths. You’ll also benefit from the backing of one of the world’s largest privately owned businesses, known for its stability and long-term focus.
In addition, you’ll receive:

Competitive remuneration package
Private health insurance
26 vacation days + flexibility
Opportunities for professional growth across the SHV group
Support to thrive within our inclusive, international culture

Local Benefits

Subsidized meals in our office canteen
Reduced working hours on Fridays and during summer
Regular company and team events

How and why to find out more
We can offer you a rewarding career with real meaning and purpose. Where you’ll feel proud of the impactful role you perform every day and the part you play in Feeding the Future. So, if you’re interested in joining us, get ready to Own your progress, your potential and your career. We aim to review all applications within ten business days and will get back to you as soon as possible.

ACCIONA, en su negocio de Facility Services selecciona un/a gestor/a de servicios para su delegación de Madrid.
Descripción del puesto
Misión:
Colaborar y apoyar en los proyectos llevados a cabo por su unidad de negocio/departamento, de acuerdo a las directrices dadas por su responsable, a fin de mejorar con su trabajo tanto los procesos de toma de decisión como los de ejecución del proyecto.

Son responsables de la ejecución de los contratos de servicios. Planifican, optimizan y supervisan la utilización de los recursos para asegurar que los servicios se ejecutan conforme a las especificaciones y coste establecidos. Entre sus funciones más comunes están: Gestionar los contratos, elaborar estimaciones presupuestarias, planificar y fijar las fases del servicio, supervisar las actividades de los subcontratistas y del personal subordinado y operarios, verificar la calidad del trabajo durante todas las fases del servicio, etc.
FUNCIONES:


Gestión integral del Servicio y las Operaciones: coordinar la apertura, cierre y operación del servicio; supervisar herramientas de gestión y cumplimiento de KPIs de servicio y productividad; mantener tiempos de respuesta adecuados y reporting al cliente.


Gestión económica del contrato: control y seguimiento de la cuenta de explotación, costes, facturación, desviaciones, etc.


Gestión de Recursos y Compras: planificar y gestionar la compra de materiales, consumibles y equipos; supervisar subcontrataciones y cumplimiento de proveedores; controlar presupuestos y corregir desviaciones.


Gestión del Personal: asegurar documentación y formación del personal; gestionar altas, bajas, cambios de jornada y relaciones laborales; monitorizar absentismo y desempeño del equipo.


Mejora Continua y Desarrollo: identificar oportunidades de mejora e impulsar planes de desarrollo, hacer propuestas de optimización del negocio; participar en estudios técnicos y económicos.


Seguridad y Prevención de Riesgos: colaborar en la gestión de seguridad y salud en el trabajo; detectar y analizar riesgos de accidentes/incidentes; coordinar reconocimientos médicos y cumplimiento de medidas de seguridad.
Requisitos del candidato
Titulación superior.
Experiencia previa de al menos 3 años en gestión de servicios de mantenimiento con personal propio.
Experiencia en gestión de equipos.
Conocimientos de RRHH y financieros.
Manejo avanzado de Excel.
Somos una compañía que valora la diversidad como fuente de talento y por ello trabajamos para impulsar un entorno inclusivo en el que se promueve el respeto, la pertenencia y el compromiso de forma que todas las personas puedan participar en igualdad de oportunidades. Invitamos a todas las personas a inscribirse con independencia del origen, circunstancias, bagaje o condición. ACCIONA ha recibido la certificación Top Employer 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024 y 2025, que reconoce a la Compañía como una de las mejores empresas para trabajar en España.

Titulo: HR Business Development Specialist – M&A and Strategic Workforce Transitions
Descripcion_Completa:
Who We Are

At Kyndryl, we run and reimagine the mission-critical technology systems that drive advantage for the world’s leading businesses. We are at the heart of progress; with proven expertise and a continuous flow of AI-powered insight, enabling smarter decisions, faster innovation, and a lasting competitive edge. For our people—Kyndryls—that means doing purposeful work that powers human progress. Join us and experience a flexible, supportive environment where your well-being is prioritized and your potential can thrive.



The Role

This role is part of the HR Business Development (HRBD) function and is responsible for leading and coordinating the HR workstream in complex business transactions such as acquisitions, divestitures, and service outsourcing. The specialist acts as a strategic advisor to business leaders and ensures seamless execution of people-related solutions across global teams and HR functions.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategic HR Advisory:

Partner with Corporate Development, Legal, and Finance to shape deal strategy from an HR perspective.

Provide input to business cases, risk assessments, and HR budget planning.

Influence decision-making by articulating people impacts and mitigation strategies.

2. Cross-Functional HR Workstream Leadership :

Lead the orchestration of HR deliverables across Talent Acquisition, Total Rewards, Payroll, ELR, HR Services, and Workday teams.

Ensure alignment of timelines, data readiness, and compliance across all HR functions.

Drive clarity on roles and responsibilities through SOPs and DTPs.

3. International Teaming & Cultural Integration:

Coordinate with regional HR leads to ensure compliance with local legislation and cultural norms.

Support leadership alignment and organisational change management to accelerate integration and productivity.

Facilitate knowledge transfer and onboarding across borders, including matrix manager assignments.

4. Collaboration with Deal Teams and Client-Facing Solutioning:

Act as the HR lead in opportunity engagement teams, working closely with deal teams, sellers, and solution architects to shape people-related offerings for clients.

Translate client requirements into executable HR solutions that align with Kyndryl’s delivery model and brand promise.

Ensure HR readiness for client transitions, including co-determination, onboarding, and compliance.

5. Divestiture and Exit Support:

Lead HR planning and execution for divestitures, including employee transfers out of Kyndryl to third parties.

Manage people-related risks, ensure compliance with local laws, and support cultural and operational transitions.

Maintain employee experience and business continuity during service exits.

6. Governance & Continuous Improvement:

Maintain and evolve the HRBD toolkit, project intake forms, and governance models.

Capture lessons learned and contribute to the development of a formal HR M&A playbook.

Monitor project health and engagement metrics to drive continuous improvement.



Who You Are

You’re good at what you do and possess the required experience to prove it. However, equally as important – you have a growth mindset; keen to drive your own personal and professional development. You are customer-focused – someone who prioritizes customer success in their work. And finally, you’re open and borderless – naturally inclusive in how you work with others.

Required Skills & Competencies

Strong understanding of HR operating models and global employment practices.

Proven ability to lead cross-functional and cross-border teams.

Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and influencing skills.

Experience with Workday, HR analytics, and project management tools.

Fluent in English

Preferred Qualifications

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Human Resources, Business, or related field.

5+ years in HR roles with exposure to all HR core processes such as compensation, payroll or employee relations

Certification in change management or M&A (e.g., Wharton M&A programme) is a plus.



Being You

The “Kyn” in Kyndryl means kinship, which represents the strong bonds we have with each other, our customers and our communities. We focus on ensuring all Kyndryls feel included and we welcome people of all cultures, backgrounds, and experiences. Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply. We believe in growth, and we’re excited to see what you can bring. At Kyndryl, employee feedback has told us that our number one driver of employee engagement is belonging. That sense of belonging — being a valued, respected, trusted member of the team — is fundamental to our culture and fueling great experiences for our customers. This dedication to welcoming everyone into our company means that Kyndryl gives you the ability to thrive and contribute to our culture of empathy and shared success. That’s The Kyndryl Way.



What You Can Expect

Your career with us isn’t just a job—it’s an adventure with purpose. We offer a dynamic, hybrid-friendly culture that supports your well-being and empowers you to grow. Our Be Well programs are thoughtfully designed to support your financial, mental, physical, and social health—because we know that when you feel your best, you do your best.

From your very first day, you’ll dive into impactful work that powers the systems our customers rely on every day. You won’t just contribute—you’ll make a difference, tackling meaningful projects that sharpen your skills and fuel your growth.

We’re here to champion your journey. With powerful tools to chart your career path, personalized development goals aligned with your ambitions, and continuous feedback to keep you inspired and on track, you’ll have everything you need to thrive and evolve. Nexthink is the leader in digital employee experience management software. Anticipate future talent needs, identifying required roles, skills, and market challenges across EMEA.

Day-to-Day Talent Operations

Guide employees and leaders through complex employee relations matters with empathy and fairness.
Collaborate with the People & Talent team to continuously improve HR operations and employee experience.

7+ years of progressive experience as an HR Business Partner or HR Leader in a multinational or tech-driven environment.

OPTIMIZE, ENABLE, INNOVATE!

Track KPIs such as time‑to‑approve, compliance adherence, request quality, and savings
Provide data‑driven insights and recommendations to senior leadership
Continuously improve governance frameworks, approval matrices, and documentation
Support audits, risk assessments, and policy updates related to external workforce management

YOU ARE A COTY FIT

Our Company

Through its Social Innovation Business that brings together IT, OT(Operational Technology) and products, Hitachi contributes to a harmonized society where the environment, wellbeing, and economic growth are in balance. Hitachi operates globally in four sectors – Digital Systems & Services, Energy, Mobility, and Connective Industries – and the Strategic SIB Business Unit for new growth businesses. With Lumada at its core, Hitachi generates value from integrating data, technology and domain knowledge to solve customer and social challenges. Revenues for FY2024 (ended March 31, 2025) totaled 9,783.3 billion yen, with 618 consolidated subsidiaries and approximately 280,000 employees worldwide. Visit us at www.hitachi.com.

The Hitachi Group has been moving towards realizing One Hitachi HR functions and talent management under its new management policy, Inspire 2027, which began in FY25. This is a very important initiative for improving our corporate value and further growth. We are looking to grow our team to help accelerate the creation of One Hitachi systems, policies, mechanisms and foundations.

The Talent Management CoE (Centre of Excellence) team at Hitachi support business growth and sustainable competitiveness by continuing to pursue the “right design'' for HR systems, policies, and processes based on expertise and a One Hitachi perspective.

What you’ll be doing

In this critical role you will have opportunity to work with HR, business leaders, and key stakeholders to help build a high‑performing workforce; designing the frameworks that help our people thrive and which support long‑term organisational growth. Collaborating with the HR teams of Hitachi Group companies globally, the Global Talent Management CoE team will implement HR strategies across the Group.

You will be directly involved in activities that strengthen the Hitachi Group's future growth and business foundation and will be able to play an active role as an HR professional. You will also be able to broaden your horizons by working as one global team, (there are 15 team members located across Asia, EMEA and the Americas) and by being exposed to world-class HR methods and expertise.

Responsibilities may include:

Mid-long term One Hitachi talent management strategy

Succession planning and talent pipeline for key positions

Talent pool management and talent

Next generation leader development

Global performance management

At Hitachi, you will be able to take advantage of the unrivalled career opportunities that come from being part of the global Hitachi group, including moving within the global CoE or into a group company.

What you bring to the team

We’re looking for a team player, who is motivated by delivering great work and the Hitachi vision. The role will be a mixture of remote-hybrid work with occasional travel to the Hitachi Office.

The ideal candidate will possess the following skills and background:

Experience in HR strategic planning and/or CoE HR

Time spent as a HRBP will be helpful

Perseverance and the ability to see work through to the end.

Ability to actively communicate with executives and stakeholders, build trusting relationships that transcend positions, and collaborate to advance workAbility

to think positively about new challenges, consider solutions from a first-person perspective, and tenaciously tackle them while involving others

Logical thinking, planning, and data analysis skills.

Open-minded communication skills to build relationships with others.

Business English proficiency. Japanese language proficiency is a bonus but not required.

Project management basics such as PMP/Prince 2, are desirable.

Our Values

We are proud to say we are an equal opportunity employer and welcome all applicants for employment without attention to any factor that doesn’t impact your ability to do the job, including race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status. We are proud of Japanese heritage, with our values expressed through the Hitachi Spirit:

Makoto – Sincerity, Fairness, Honesty, Integrity

Kaitakusha-Seishin – Pioneering Spirit, Challenge
