Job Description:
Do you dare to lead the digital transformation to create a more sustainable world?
At Schneider Electric, we are the leading digital partner in sustainability and efficiency for businesses, communities, and individuals. Our goal is for all people to be able to make the most of our energy and resources through the integration of processes, software, big data, and energy technologies that enable integrated management of buildings, industries, and cities. We are present in over 110 countries with a diverse and disruptive team of over 150,000 people. If you are seeking an opportunity to propel your career and challenge your creativity, we want to meet you!

Schneider Digital is the digital department of Schneider Electric, leading the digital transformation in the company by giving support globally to our internal teams and our clients. Schneider Digital consists of 6 Digital Hubs worldwide which are strategically located to ensure a 24/7 support across the company (France, China, India, USA, Mexico and Spain). Our Digital Hub in Barcelona is formed by +450 employees working in strategic projects and different roles such as Data, Cybersecurity, ERP, Cloud, Infrastructures, IT Project Management or Digital Marketing.

We are looking for an HR Cybersecurity Compliance Analyst to join our team. You will be part of the dynamic Schneider Digital – Employee Experience team whose mission is to “Create digital workplace of the future & Enable HR to build workforce of the future”. Within this team, you will be part of the Cybersecurity and Compliance team to oversee the effective enforcement of Cybersecurity controls on applications that support HR Processes.

What will you do?
As a Cybersecurity and Compliance Analyst, your main role is to support Cyber Security, and Privacy compliance for HR processes and supporting applications.
To safeguard sensitive HR data and ensure regulatory compliance by proactively identifying risks, implementing robust security controls, and fostering a culture of data protection.
This role bridges cybersecurity expertise with HR-specific needs, ensuring that employee data, systems, and processes are secure, compliant, and resilient against threats.

Main responsibilities include:
Data Protection & Privacy
Ensure HR systems comply with data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA, local labor laws).
Monitor and enforce policies for handling Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and sensitive employee data.
Risk Assessment & Mitigation
Support risk assessments on HR applications (e.g., payroll, recruitment platforms, employee portals).
Identify vulnerabilities and recommend mitigation strategies tailored to HR workflows.
Collaborate with HR and IT teams to implement secure configurations and access controls.
Compliance Monitoring
Track and report on compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
Maintain documentation for audits and regulatory reviews.
System & Application Security
Collaborate with IT to secure HRIS, ATS, LMS, and other HR platforms.
Ensure encryption, authentication, and role-based access controls are properly implemented.
Participate in change management processes for HR systems to assess security impact.



Qualifications:
What qualifications will make you successful for this role?
Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
Solid understanding of cybersecurity principles, data privacy laws, and compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, NIS 2, HIPAA, SOC 2).
Experience securing HR systems such as Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM, or similar platforms.
Proficiency with security tools (e.g., SIEM, DLP, IAM, GRC platforms).
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Effective communication and stakeholder management, especially with HR and legal teams.
Ability to translate technical risks into business impact for non-technical audiences.
Proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and collaboration.
3–5 years of experience in cybersecurity, IT audit, or compliance roles.
Demonstrated experience working with HR or other sensitive data domains.

Desired/Preferred to have.
Preferred certifications (any of the following):
Certified Information Systems Security Professional (CISSP)
Certified Information Security Manager (CISM)
Certified Information Privacy Professional (CIPP/US, CIPP/E)
ISO/IEC 27001 Lead Implementer or similar

Location: Spain (Barcelona)


What will you get?

We adapt to you:

With our flexible schedule, you'll have the freedom to adjust your work hours to accommodate your personal needs and responsibilities.

We know how great it is to work from home. With our hybrid work plan, you can enjoy working from the comfort of your home.

Need more time to relax and disconnect? With our Holy Pack, you can purchase additional vacation days to recharge when you need it most.

Celebrate holidays your way! With Floating Holidays, you can exchange holidays for other days that better suit your plans and personal preferences.

‍Need additional time to explore new experiences or focus on personal projects? With the Sabbatical Pack, you can request up to 2 months of extra leave (unpaid).

We value the importance of family and want to support you in all aspects of your life. Our Global Family Leave Policy provides flexible paid conditions for when you need it most, whether you're about to be a parent, need time to overcome a loss, or to care for a loved one.


We take care of you:

With access to the health and wellness platform Wellwo, you and up to 5 people of your choice can enjoy access to wellness content, nutrition counseling, fitness classes, and more!

‍From dance classes to booking a hotel spa, we offer an agreement with Wellhub so you can access a network of thousands of gyms and sports centers to keep your body active in the way you like best.

Your well-being is our priority. You will have medical service at your workplace for close attention to any medical needs.


We empower you:

With Open Talent Market, our AI-driven professional development platform, you can connect with job opportunities, projects, and mentors at Schneider Electric globally.

We offer you the opportunity to be a shareholder of Schneider Electric and share in our achievements with our stock ownership program.

We celebrate everyone's talent and success with our recognition program, through which you can give and receive points for your achievements and redeem them for gift cards at your favorite stores.

Overview:
Are you interested in joining a dynamic and international team of professionals to transform education? IE University's IT department is looking for a IT Business Partner - Dynamics 365 FO & HR to join its team!


The IT Business Partner - Dynamics 365 FO & HR will act as a strategic interface between IT and the Finance and HR departments, aligning business needs with IT initiatives. This role involves managing stakeholder relationships, translating business requirements into IT projects, overseeing the delivery of IT solutions, mitigating risks, communicating changes, monitoring budgets, and driving continuous improvement.


Why IE University?
With 50 years of experience and founded by and for entrepreneurs, IE University has turned business education on its head through disruptive teaching. We count on high-level faculty who bring real-world experience into the classroom and foster a hands-on approach to learning.
Our Purpose
At IE University, we foster positive change through education, research, and innovation.
As a transformative educational institution, we empower students to make their mark on the world. We provide them with the skills, knowledge and environment needed to make a difference, and they join a supportive and international community that lasts a lifetime.
Our spirit of innovation and entrepreneurship has allowed us to drive change and constantly stay at the cutting edge of higher education. And through diversity, we have harnessed the power of people while also understanding and embracing the importance of humanities.
Our People
At IE we are:
People-driven
Change Builders
Autonomy Enthusiasts
Communication Enthusiasts
Knowledge Champions
We are looking for a person to join a full-time position in Madrid - IE Tower
Responsibilities:
Your role as IT Business Partner - Dynamics 365 FO & HR will involve:
Build and maintain strong relationships with Finance and HR departments to understand their goals, objectives, and operational needs.
Translate business requirements into actionable IT projects and solutions.
Act as the primary liaison between IT and the business areas, ensuring seamless communication and collaboration.
Provide a holistic perspective on cross-departmental processes to identify synergies and align initiatives.
Communicate IT priorities, capabilities, and constraints effectively to stakeholders.
Ensure IT solutions are designed, implemented, and maintained to meet business requirements and expectations.
Contribute to the planning, design, and execution of IT solutions and projects.
Coordinate with IT teams to ensure timely and successful project delivery.
Perform User Acceptance Testing (UAT) of IT solutions prior to involving business stakeholders to ensure quality and alignment with requirements.
Identify and manage risks associated with IT projects and initiatives.
Effectively communicate system changes, project updates, and timelines to stakeholders.
Provide training and support to business users to facilitate the adoption of new technologies and processes.
Monitor IT spending and ensure alignment with budget allocations.
Proactively identify opportunities for innovation, process optimization, and technology-driven improvements.
Qualifications:
To thrive in this role, you should possess the following qualifications:
Education:
A university degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Administration).
A master’s degree is highly desirable.
Experience: Minimum of 6 years of experience in IT or consulting, with proven expertise in managing IT projects supporting Finance and HR functions.
Knowledge:
Extensive experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (FO) and Human Resources (HR).
Expertise in other ERP or HR solutions (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Workday) will be highly valued.
Language Skills: High proficiency in English; fluency in Spanish is highly desirable.
Skills:
Strong communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills.
Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Visionary and strategic thinking with the ability to drive innovation and change.
Exceptional organizational skills to manage multiple tasks and priorities effectively.
Attributes:
Customer-focused mindset with a commitment to delivering high-quality solutions.
Creative, proactive, and adaptable to evolving business needs and technologies.
Unleash your full potential and make an impact. The next move is yours!

If you want to be part of this educational revolution, we invite you to apply and be part of our dynamic team at IE University.
At IE University, we actively promote equal opportunities in recruitment processes and access to employment, always ensuring the absence of gender, sexual orientation, religion, age, ethnic origin, or disability bias throughout the entire selection process.

Job Title:




TCO Senior Business Consultant






Department:




Business Consulting (Business Advisory Services)






Location:




France




JOB PURPOSE & KEY OBJECTIVES:

The TCO (Total cost of ownership) Senior Business Consultant plays a crucial role in the sales process by working closely with the sales and business consulting teams, prospective clients, and internal stakeholders to analyse and communicate the long-term financial benefits of becoming an ADP client. This position involves creating and presenting TCO studies that help prospects understand the full scope of costs associated with In-Country and Global Payroll over time and make informed decisions.

By leveraging their consulting expertise and a proven TCO methodology, the Senior Business Consultant assists sales teams with data and insights to drive an increase in prospective client’s perception of ADP’s value proposition.

The Senior Business Consultant will also contribute to the continuous improvement of the TCO practice. Both by researching internal and external resources for insights, but also by supporting the ongoing maintenance of the materials used to build and support the TCO studies, including the Benchmark database and the team’s Business Insights/Value Drivers Library.

They will need to build an effective personal network of contacts within other ADP functions for cross-functional collaboration: including ADP Research Institute, Product Development, Sales Operations and Marketing, Area Product Leaders (APLs) to name just a few, to gather business insights and latest trends/whitepapers and information to improve our TCO approach and collateral.

As part of the BAS (Business Advisory Services) team, the Senior Business Consultant will often work directly with Field and COE (Center of Excellence), BOB and Multi-country Business Consultants, local field TCO champions, Sales DMs (District Managers) and KAMs (Key Account Managers) to support on TCO activity.

In addition to this role and as an expert of ADP products, the Senior Business Consultant will also produce recordings and documentation that will be used as TCO training material/guidelines for Sales DMs/KAMs and ESI Business Consultants.

KEY DUTIES/TASKS:

Present the TCO methodology
Create and present targeted TCO studies to support sales
Maintain BOB and MNC Benchmark Database and work with fellow BAS Consultants to extract and present business insights for use in the sales process
Provide benchmark data to support campaigns whitepapers and marketing/sales collateral
Support the creation of high-level TCO tools/quick calculators and TCO collateral and develop training material including demo recordings to train others
Chase down insights and TCO metrics from other ADP teams as required for the TCO/value proposition (e.g., marketing, product, security & compliance, implementation, R&D, etc.…) and incorporate insights to improve the TCO approach and Sales message
Build demo recordings and publish them to Sales and BCs on ADP Works
Manage content improvement process on TCO offerings and Business Insights
Develop training methodology, documentation and provide ongoing support for TCO Estimator champions in the Business Consulting community
Lead projects to automate the Multidomain TCO calculator – like the project related to the Payroll TCO Estimator
Develop/Create standard client facing collaterals for the HR domain – needed to support generating more Sales Revenue on additional offering along with payroll.
Improve the TCO Multidomain Calculator (e.g., automating the pricing input and the pricing calculations, reducing manual work and the risk of errors due to user input)
Lead projects for new TCO tools (e.g., continuous UAT of the tool, track necessary changes, lead the communication with developers, organize and lead the project team meetings – establish priority of changes, track timelines and deadlines)

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:

Language:

French: native speaker/writer

English: native speaker/writer or fluent (C2)

Additional languages are an advantage

Should have good level of skill in technical writing and speaking in English. They should be able to communicate with the rest of the TCO team and other Business consultants that they work with, summarize or rephrase some data provided by subject matter expert or existing content, depending on the level of details required for inclusion in the TCO/Business Insights. Should also be able to communicate with leadership team on TCO/Business Insights presentations, collateral and progress.

Must be able to converse with diverse audiences (typically functional HR and Financial controllers) and appropriately adjust BAS content to meet differing knowledge and interest, and juggling meetings with multiple audience levels.

Experience and qualifications:

Work experience:

3-5 years of experience in similar roles (pre-sales, sales assistant, business analyst …)
High level of mastery of MS Excel (advanced formula & pivot tables) and PowerPoint is mandatory. Client presentations are expected.
Consulting, finance, payroll or Human Resources background is a plus
Experience with data science, data gathering, data consolidation and building a business case/value proposition is a big plus
Experience of working on multinational projects or in a multinational function is a plus
Experience in a sales, implementation or client service role is also a plus.

ESSENTIAL COMPETENCIES

Exceptional written and oral customer service and communication skills required.
Is analytical and capable of building a big picture assessment from detailed component inputs
Ability to influence the actions of others.
Ability to work under pressure while meeting deadlines.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to effectively prioritize work and multi-task.
Well-developed interpersonal skills, with an ability to communicate well at all organizational levels.
Project Management Experience
Is resourceful, self-motivated, prospect/client focused
Highly valued interpersonal skills include the ability to be rational, objective and unbiased when making decisions or taking action
Enthusiastic, committed and able to remain calm under pressure and deliver to tight deadlines
Has the ability to encourage others to deliver/respond when faced with their apathy or low prioritization
Ability to travel for client on site meetings if required

Titulo: Global Lead - HR Compliance, Reporting and Mobility
Descripcion_Completa:
Your role at Dynatrace

Dynatrace seeks a knowledgeable and globally experienced Strategic Lead for HR Compliance, Reporting and Mobility. You will lead on defining the strategy, developing and execute our global compliance reporting and global mobility programs.

This role is pivotal in overseeing the development, implementation, and management of the company's employee compliance reporting requirement, ensuring adherence to international employment laws, immigration regulations, reporting requirements, and internal Dynatrace policies and practices, while providing accurate and timely reporting to senior management and regulatory bodies.

We are looking for individuals who will lead and conduct risk assessments, foster process automation, and collaborate closely with People & Culture Centres of Excellence, internal and external Counsels across EMEA, APAC, NORAM, and LATAM.

Hybrid and remote working conditions considered.

Specific Responsibilities:

Design, implement, and maintain comprehensive P&C compliance reporting framework that meets regulatory and internal audit standards.
Ensure timely and accurate submission of compliance reports to federal, state, provincial, and local regulatory agencies, maintaining adherence to employment laws across all regions.
Continuously monitor global employment legislation and assess its impact on compliance reporting and mobility practices.
Lead P&C risk assessments, represent the function in internal & external audits, respond to vendor and certification requests, and manage global compliance documentation.
Partner with P&C Centers of Excellence (COEs) to ensure data accuracy and consistency for compliance reporting and mobility tracking.
Develop and maintain dashboards and analytics to monitor compliance, KPI’s, and global mobility trends.
Establish reporting systems to track and analyze compliance performance and identify areas for improvement.
Maintain detailed records of compliance activities, including audits, investigations, international assignments, visa sponsorships, and cross-border employment structures.
Collaborate with Legal, Payroll, People Operations, P&C COEs, P&C Business Partners, Business Systems, and external vendors to ensure compliant relocation, expatriation, and repatriation processes.
Serve as a trusted advisor to regional P&C teams, COEs, Leadership, Legal, and Finance on compliance reporting and mobility matters.
Create and deliver training programs to educate employees, people leaders and P&C colleagues on compliance protocols, policies, and responsibilities.
Foster cross-functional relationships to ensure a unified approach to compliance and act as a key point of contact for compliance-related inquiries.
Define and lead compliance strategies, metrics, policies, and automation strategies for P&C ER reporting.
Review and assess P&C policies for possible risks, and maintain a robust compliance reporting framework.
Research complex issues, provide recommendations, and advise the organization based on precedent, policy, and regulatory requirements.
Identify and support opportunities to enhance automation, transparency, and accuracy in compliance processes and reporting.
What will help you succeed
Bachelor’s degree in Human Resource, Business Law, or related field; Master’s or JD preferred.
Minimum of 8+ years of professional experience in HR, compliance, global mobility, or employment law, preferably in a multinational environment.
English business fluent; Other languages, esp. German and Spanish, favorable.
Sound knowledge of global compliance and reporting requirements. In-depth knowledge of employment laws, regulations, and best practices.
Certificate Compliance Officer favorable.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills required. Experience in Excel, SuccessFactors, and Power BI favorable.
Sound technical skills and advanced automation experience. Project management experience favorable.
Positive role model and strong work ethic. Commitment to the highest standards of excellence and integrity.
Excellent communication, consultative, coaching, and interpersonal skills to drive cross-border collaboration and navigate complex regulatory environments.
Able to independently juggle multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously.




Why you will love being a Dynatracer
A one-product software company creating real value for the largest enterprises and millions of end customers globally, striving for a world where software works perfectly.
Working with the latest technologies and at the forefront of innovation in tech on scale; but also, in other areas like marketing, design, or research.
A team that thinks outside the box, welcomes unconventional ideas, and pushes boundaries.
An environment that fosters innovation, enables creative collaboration, and allows you to grow.
A globally unique and tailor-made career development program recognizing your potential, promoting your strengths, and supporting you in achieving your career goals.
A truly international mindset with Dynatracers from different countries & cultures all over the world, and English as the corporate language that connects us all
A culture that is being shaped by the diverse personalities, expertise, and backgrounds of our global team.

Ready to join the future of work? Join our customer as a Spanish Payroll Manager with a leading HR platform company that's building a world without borders. They make it easy to hire and manage global teams while ensuring compliance and streamlining processes for remote-first companies and digital nomads alike.

Our customer is continuously growing their talent pipeline with exceptional payroll professionals. This role is responsible for owning the complete Spanish payroll cycle and leading the strategic transition from external vendor to fully in-house payroll operations. As they value work-life balance, please only apply if you're based within 2 hours of the Central European Timezone.

We believe in building relationships with top talent and would welcome your application to be considered for upcoming positions within their expanding team. If you're passionate about Spanish payroll and interested in being part of this exciting journey, don't hesitate to reach out! Your next career adventure might be just around the corner.

What you will do as a Spanish Payroll Manager...

Own and run the full, end-to-end Spanish payroll cycle independently – from data collection and validation through to calculations, controls, submissions, payments and reporting.

Lead and manage the implementation/in-housing of Spanish payroll from current external vendor, including planning, data migration, testing, parallel runs and go-live processes.

Manage all associated payroll activities in Spain, including starters and leavers, contract changes, benefits and deductions, year-end processes, reconciliations and reporting to Spanish authorities.

Act as the main point of contact for Spanish payroll, resolving employee and client issues while clearly explaining payroll topics to non-experts across the organization.

Ensure strict compliance with Spanish payroll legislation and statutory requirements (Seguridad Social, IRPF, mandatory insurances, pensions, etc.) by correctly applying existing rules in daily operations.

Review and improve payroll processes and controls, documenting procedures and identifying opportunities for standardization and automation across the European payroll function.

What we are looking for…

Minimum 5 years' hands-on Spanish payroll experience, with proven end-to-end ownership of the full payroll cycle in in-house environments.

Demonstrable experience managing or significantly contributing to the transition of Spanish payroll from an outsourced vendor to an in-house model, including planning, system sourcing, setup and stakeholder coordination.

Strong, up-to-date working knowledge of Spanish payroll legislation and statutory requirements (social security, IRPF, benefits, mandatory insurances, etc.) with the ability to apply these accurately in practice.

Proven ability to run Spanish payroll 100% independently, aligning with internal policies and controls, without day-to-day technical supervision or support.

Advanced proficiency with Spanish payroll systems/tools and MS Excel, confident with reconciliations, data checks and troubleshooting discrepancies.

Fluent Spanish and strong English communication skills, comfortable working with Spanish authorities, vendors and international internal stakeholders.

Desirable (but not required):

Experience working in a multi-country or multi-entity payroll environment, ideally across Europe with vendor management experience.

Experience in fast-growing or changing environments, where processes and systems are being built and improved over time.

Previous experience in an EOR/PEO, global employment, or HR tech environment.

Experience working with Google Workspace (Sheets, Docs, Gmail) and collaborative documentation tools like Confluence.

Startup environment experience with hands-on, roll-up-the-sleeves mentality.

Benefits and perks…

Global, remote, and thriving: Join a global team of talented individuals spread across 26 countries. The flexible work program lets you work from anywhere, anytime. Whether it's a beachside office or a cosy home office, the choice is yours.

Your well-being is our priority: Mental health matters, which is why they offer a dedicated Well-Being Day - a full day off just to recharge and relax.

Get fit and stay active: Whether it's yoga, weightlifting, or a quick jog, work out on your terms, at a discount with subsidised gym memberships.

Learn, grow, and develop: They believe in constant growth. With the Training & Development Allowance, you'll have all the opportunities you need to keep expanding your skills and knowledge.

Stay connected: Take part in exciting annual team meetups!

Diversity is our superpower: They are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.

Benefits vary depending on location due to local laws and regulations.

We’re looking for: HR People Operations Specialist

The Role

We’re currently looking for an HR People Operations Specialist to join our remote-first, globally distributed team.

In this role, you will help build and run the operational backbone of our People function—coordinating day-to-day HR processes, maintaining accurate records, supporting onboarding and offboarding, and ensuring smooth collaboration between HR, Finance, and leadership.

This position is ideal for someone who enjoys structure, is comfortable with systems and data, and cares deeply about creating a seamless experience for our team members around the world.

Ability to work independently with minimum supervision, comfortable in a fully remote, asynchronous environment

At least 3–5 years of experience in HR operations, people & engagement, or project coordination (or a combination of these)
Experience working with HR systems, spreadsheets, and collaborative tools
Good understanding of core HR processes, policies and standards (onboarding/offboarding, records management, time-off tracking, basic compliance)
